name: AbbVie Inc.
business: AbbVie is a global, diversified research-based biopharmaceutical company with a comprehensive product portfolio in immunology, neuroscience, oncology, and aesthetics. The company sells its products worldwide directly to wholesalers, distributors, government agencies, health care facilities, specialty pharmacies, and independent retailers. In the United States, AbbVie distributes pharmaceutical products principally through independent wholesale distributors, with some sales directly to retailers, pharmacies, patients or other customers.

exposures.sentence: AbbVie uses its expertise, dedicated people and unique approach to innovation to develop and market advanced therapies that address some of the world s most complex and serious diseases.

AbbVie s Aesthetics portfolio consists of facial injectables, plastics and regenerative medicine, body contouring and skincare products, which hold market-leading positions in the United States and in key markets around the world.

AbbVie intends to execute its strategy and advance its mission in a number of ways, including: ... continuing to invest in and expand its pipeline in support of opportunities across our core areas of immunology, neuroscience, oncology and aesthetics as well as new sources of growth such as obesity.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of Rinvoq for giant cell arteritis in adults
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, AbbVie announced that the U.S. FDA approved Rinvoq for the treatment of GCA in adult patients.

catalysts.event: Positive topline results from Phase 3 UP-AA trial for Rinvoq in severe alopecia areata (Study 2)
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, AbbVie announced positive topline results from Study 2 of its Phase 3 UP-AA trial for Rinvoq as a monotherapy in adults and adolescents with severe alopecia areata (AA).

catalysts.event: Positive topline results from Phase 3 UP-AA trial for Rinvoq (Study 1)
catalysts.sentence: In August 2025, AbbVie announced positive topline results from Study 1 of its Phase 3 UP-AA trial for Rinvoq as a monotherapy in adult and adolescent patients with severe AA.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of sNDA for Rinvoq in ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, AbbVie announced that the U.S. FDA approved a supplemental New Drug Application (sNDA) that updates the indication statement for Rinvoq for the treatment of adults with moderately to severely active ulcerative colitis and moderately to severely active Crohn's disease.

catalysts.event: Positive topline results from Phase 3b/4 SELECT-SWITCH study for Rinvoq vs Humira
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, AbbVie announced positive topline results from the Phase 3b/4 head-to-head SELECT-SWITCH study evaluating the efficacy and safety of Rinvoq compared to Humira in adult patients with moderate to severe rheumatoid arthritis (RA), who had an inadequate response or intolerance to a single TNF inhibitor other than Humira.

catalysts.event: Positive topline results from Phase 3 studies for Rinvoq in vitiligo
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, AbbVie announced positive topline results from two replicate Phase 3 studies evaluating the efficacy and safety of Rinvoq in adult and adolescent patients with non-segmental vitiligo.

catalysts.event: MAA submission for Rinvoq in severe alopecia areata to EMA
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, AbbVie submitted a marketing authorization application (MAA) to the European Medicines Agency (EMA) for Rinvoq for the treatment of adults and adolescents 12 years and older with severe AA.

catalysts.event: Submission of applications for Rinvoq in vitiligo to FDA and EMA
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, AbbVie announced the submission of applications for a new indication to the U.S. FDA and EMA for Rinvoq for the treatment of adult and adolescent patients with non-segmental vitiligo.

catalysts.event: Phase 3 clinical trial initiation for Qulipta in menstrual migraine
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, AbbVie initiated a Phase 3 clinical trial to evaluate Qulipta for the preventive treatment of menstrual migraine.

catalysts.event: Positive topline results from Phase 3 TEMPLE study for Qulipta vs topiramate
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, AbbVie announced positive topline results from its Phase 3 TEMPLE head-to-head study evaluating the tolerability, safety and efficacy of Qulipta compared to the highest tolerated dose of topiramate in adult patients with a history of four or more migraine days per month.

catalysts.event: Positive topline results from Phase 3 ECLIPSE study for Aquipta in acute migraine
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, AbbVie announced results from the Phase 3 ECLIPSE study, evaluating the safety, efficacy and tolerability of Aquipta versus placebo for the acute treatment of migraine in adults.

catalysts.event: Submission of application for Aquipta for acute migraine to EMA
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, AbbVie announced the submission of an application for a new indication to the EMA for Aquipta for the acute treatment of adult patients with migraine.

catalysts.event: NDA submission for tavapadon for Parkinson's disease to FDA
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, AbbVie announced the submission of a New Drug Application (NDA) to the U.S. FDA for tavapadon, a novel selective dopamine D1/D5 receptor partial agonist, for the treatment of Parkinson's disease.

catalysts.event: FDA accelerated approval for Emrelis in non-small cell lung cancer
catalysts.sentence: In May 2025, AbbVie announced that the U.S. FDA granted accelerated approval for Emrelis (telisotuzumab vedotin-tllv) for the treatment of adult patients with locally advanced or metastatic, non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer with high c-Met protein overexpression who have received a prior systemic therapy.

catalysts.event: Global Phase 3 VERONA trial for Venclexta in MDS did not meet primary endpoint
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, AbbVie announced that the global Phase 3 VERONA trial evaluating Venclexta in combination with azacitidine in the treatment of newly diagnosed higher-risk myelodysplastic syndrome did not meet the primary endpoint of overall survival.

catalysts.event: sNDA submission for Venclexta + acalabrutinib in CLL to FDA
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, AbbVie announced the submission of a sNDA to the U.S. FDA for the fixed-duration, all oral combination regimen of Venclexta and acalabrutinib in previously untreated patients with chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL).

catalysts.event: Positive topline results from Phase 3 trial for Epkinly in follicular lymphoma
catalysts.sentence: In May 2025, Genmab A/S (Genmab) announced positive topline results from the Phase 3 trial evaluating Epkinly plus rituximab and lenalidomide versus rituximab and lenalidomide alone in adult patients with relapsed or refractory (R/R) follicular lymphoma.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of Epkinly plus rituximab and lenalidomide for R/R follicular lymphoma
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, AbbVie announced that the U.S. FDA approved Epkinly plus rituximab and lenalidomide for the treatment of adult patients with R/R follicular lymphoma.

catalysts.event: Topline results from Phase 3 trial for Epkinly in DLBCL
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, AbbVie announced topline results from the Phase 3 trial evaluating Epkinly compared to investigator's choice of chemoimmunotherapy in adult patients with R/R diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL).

catalysts.event: BLA submission for pivekimab sunirine (PVEK) for BPDCN to FDA
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, AbbVie announced the submission of a BLA to the U.S. FDA for approval of pivekimab sunirine (PVEK), an investigational antibody-drug conjugate (ADC), for treatment of blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm (BPDCN).

catalysts.event: BLA submission for trenibotulinumtoxinE for glabellar lines to FDA
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, AbbVie announced the submission of a BLA to the U.S. FDA for approval of trenibotulinumtoxinE (TrenibotE) for the treatment of moderate to severe glabellar lines.

catalysts.event: FDA acceptance of supplemental PMA for Skinvive by Juvederm for neck lines
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, AbbVie announced that the U.S. FDA accepted for review the supplemental premarket approval application for Skinvive by Juvederm to reduce neck lines for the improvement of neck appearance.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of Emblaveo for complicated intra-abdominal infections
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, AbbVie announced that the U.S. FDA approved Emblaveo (aztreonam and avibactam), as the first fixed-dose, intravenous, monobactam/ -lactamase inhibitor combination antibiotic to treat complicated intra-abdominal infections, including those caused by Gram-negative bacteria.

catalysts.event: FDA label expansion for Mavyret in hepatitis C
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, AbbVie announced that the U.S. FDA approved a label expansion for Mavyret, an oral pangenotypic direct acting antiviral therapy. It is now approved for the treatment of adults and pediatric patients three years and older with acute or chronic hepatitis C virus infection.

catalysts.event: Settlement of litigation with generic manufacturers for Rinvoq tablets
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, AbbVie announced the settlement of litigation with all generic manufacturers that filed abbreviated new drug applications with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for generic versions of upadacitinib tablets, which AbbVie markets as Rinvoq.
